title: campanula rhomboidalis var. lanceolata
intro: Campanula rhomboidalis var. lanceolata, also known as the Alpine Bellflower, is a type of flowering plant in the bellflower family, native to the mountainous regions of the Balkans. The medicinal properties of Campanula rhomboidalis var. lanceolata include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
